hypothesis: G22 standard50: a closed-option selector that sees raw deterministic candidates plus prediction-time G21-style constructed options can improve Gan S0 target selection on gan_s0_g6_standard50_v1 versus builder-gap GPT, D1 v1.2b, G8, G10, and G15 without changing scorer, loader, split, bridge, candidate-builder, constructor, label-construction, or prediction-repair semantics.
metric_caveats: G22 varies target selection over a closed option surface only; the model must select an option ID and cannot free-write a final label.
This run uses gan_s0_g6_standard50_v1 for mechanism comparison; it is not an unbiased validation estimate and cannot replace full synthetic validation.
Gan gold is check__Seizure Frequency Number.seizure_frequency_number[0]; reference[0] is retained only as a difficulty signal.

Constructed options are generated from note text at prediction time by gan.frequency.aggregation_constructor.v1 in open-attempt mode, de-duplicated against raw candidate labels, and are not gated by gold, reference labels, G19 row classes, or G17 buckets.
Post-hoc G19/G17/G21 row ledgers are reporting overlays only and are not visible to the model.
Run-level metrics use gan2026_paper_reproduction with repair/range/tolerance disabled; canonical gan_frequency_deterministic_v1 is diagnostic sensitivity only.

Do not full-validate unless this standard50 mechanism clears the G22 stop rule: at least a two-record paper-monthly lift over the best current standard50 baseline and no motivating-overlay regression.
